helpful: Full petition and brief-in-opposition text were decisive — the BIO's preservation argument materially moved the estimate and is invisible from the snapshot alone.
Per-Term paid-vs-IFP grant rates in statpack.json gave the right fee-class anchor.
The snapshot's proceedings list cleanly surfaced the call-for-response after a waiver, the strongest docket signal.
gaps: This statpack build has no 'Segment base rate by salience band' table (the prompt points to it) and no call-for-response cut; CFR is the dominant live signal on paid petitions and I had to calibrate its effect from general knowledge rather than corpus data.

fedcourts query returned zero rows for citation-based (Jones, Burger) and topic-based (Fourth Amendment) filters on scotus/2020s, so corpus priors contributed nothing; either the topic vocabulary or the citation index does not cover cert-stage petitions well.
notes: Forward-mode cell with rich provisioned documents; no MCP or web retrieval needed.
